headline: Rare Disease Patients Face Average 5.2-Year Diagnostic Delay
context: Malaysian rare disease patients wait an average of 5.2 years from symptom onset to accurate diagnosis.

cards.big: Rare disease patients wait 5.2 years on average before receiving correct diagnosis
cards.sub: Malaysia has an estimated 300,000 rare disease patients across 7,000 conditions

cards.big: Only 2 public hospitals have dedicated rare disease units as of 2025
cards.sub: Most rare disease patients are managed by non-specialist doctors without expertise

cards.big: National Rare Disease Policy adopted in 2023 has no dedicated funding mechanism
cards.sub: Policy without budget allocation remains aspirational rather than operational

cards.big: Australia's Rare Disease Action Plan includes RM180 million for genomic diagnosis access
cards.sub: Funded genomic testing reduces diagnostic odyssey from years to weeks

cards.big: Rare diseases are individually uncommon but collectively affect more people than diabetes
Should Malaysia fund genomic diagnostic access for all suspected rare disease patients
